proof_or_counterexample_mode: exact signed residual decomposition plus norm-only lower-bound falsification
attempt: Turn TICKET93's Type II requirement into an explicit signed remainder budget and test whether L2 approximation alone can close it.

exact_decomposition.coordinate: Lambda=alpha_R Lambda_R+E_R, with alpha_R chosen by least squares on the audited interval.
exact_decomposition.signed_remainder: D_R=alpha_R<A,E_shift>+alpha_R<E,A_shift>+<E,E_shift> must be bounded from below jointly.

norm_only_no_go.audit_result: Every tested truncation has positive exact correlation but a negative norm-only lower bound.
norm_only_no_go.logical_reason: Norm information is invariant under adversarial residual sign changes and cannot recover the required one-sided shifted cancellation unless the residual is much smaller than observed.
norm_only_no_go.consequence: A useful theorem must estimate the combined signed remainder D_R, not its three terms by absolute value.

discarded_routes.counteredge: All four certified norm-only lower bounds are negative.
Small unshifted L2 error does not determine the sign of shifted residual correlations.

twin_signed_remainder_audit.retained_route: Estimate D_R as one signed Type II object using arithmetic cancellation before applying absolute values.
twin_signed_remainder_audit.candidate_theorem: JointShiftTwoSignedRemainderLowerBound: D_R(x)>=-alpha_R^2 S_R(x)+B(x)+omega(x) with omega(x) unbounded.
twin_signed_remainder_audit.proof_boundary: TICKET94 proves an exact decomposition and a norm-only proof-route no-go. It does not prove the signed remainder lower bound or Twin Prime.

obstruction: Every tested truncation has positive exact correlation but a negative norm-only lower bound.
candidate_theorem: JointShiftTwoSignedRemainderLowerBound: D_R(x)>=-alpha_R^2 S_R(x)+B(x)+omega(x) with omega(x) unbounded.
next_experiment: Build TICKET95 around a joint signed remainder estimate before Cauchy separation.
claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified last-twin counterexample.
